preamble: Monophthongization of the diphthong [ui] to the open front unrounded vowel [a] through vowel merger and reduction. This shift occurs in various Romance and Sino-Tibetan languages, representing a common pathway in diphthong simplification.

examples.note: In Wu Chinese dialects, the diphthong [ui] monophthongizes to [a] in certain phonological contexts.
In some Romance dialects, the diphthong [ui] reduces to open vowel [a] in rapid speech and casual registers.
